title: Crispy Mexican Sweet Potato Hash
description: Earthy, crumbly, post-workout Mexican dish that caramelizes sweet potatoes with bold spices and a splash of Tabasco for heat, all egg-free and perfect for a protein-packed meal.
instructions: Heat a large non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat. Add olive oil and let it shimmer.
Add diced sweet potatoes, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, until they begin to brown, about 5 minutes.
Toss in diced onion and red bell pepper, and continue cooking until the vegetables are tender and the sweet potatoes are caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and chili powder; cook for 1 minute until the spices are fragrant.
Add black beans and stir to combine, cooking for 2 minutes until the beans are heated through.
Drizzle Tabasco sauce and fresh lime juice over the hash, stirring to coat everything evenly. Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt, pepper, or Tabasco if desired.
Remove from heat and sprinkle chopped fresh cilantro over the top, giving it a gentle stir.
Plate the hash onto a serving dish and optionally top with a sprinkle of crushed tortilla chips for extra crunch.
